facade_description: House with white siding, Bellevue area presents a house-form facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a gable roof, consistent with its house-form typology. The facade reads primarily as wood clapboard in a white painted horizontal wood siding; some exposed red brick visible at lower level and on adjacent house palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The visible opening pattern is organized as 2, 3, 1 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance. Character-defining facade elements include cornice.
